Although the exact conditions for map switching is not entirely known, some of it is based on whether the ignition switch is on or off and whether the motor has begun to start or not at any given time in the cranking process. TGV status may also be involved. WARNING: UNTESTED

table.name: Cranking Fuel IPW Compensation Imm. Cruise (RPM)
table.description: This is the change to the 'Cranking Fuel Injector Pulse Width (ECT)', based on engine speed and coolant temp. Cruise and non-cruise conditions are determined by a number of factors including engine speed, requested torque, MAF, vehicle speed, IAT, idle mode, ECT, and SI-Drive mode (if applicable). Note: This is based on the immediate conditions related to cruise/non-cruise and results in an immediate switch, not the ramping behavior inherent with other tables when switching.

table.name: Cranking Fuel IPW Compensation Imm. Non-Cruise (RPM)
table.description: This is the change to the 'Cranking Fuel Injector Pulse Width (ECT)', based on engine speed and coolant temp. Cruise and non-cruise conditions are determined by a number of factors including engine speed, requested torque, MAF, vehicle speed, IAT, idle mode, ECT, and SI-Drive mode (if applicable). Note: This is based on the immediate conditions related to cruise/non-cruise and results in an immediate switch, not the ramping behavior inherent with other tables when switching.

table.name: Cranking Fuel IPW Compensation (MAP)
table.description: This is the change to the 'Cranking Fuel Injector Pulse Width (ECT)', based on the manifold absolute pressure.

table.name: Cranking Fuel IPW Compensation (Accelerator)
table.description: This is the change to the 'Cranking Fuel Injector Pulse Width (ECT)', based on the accelerator pedal.

table.name: Cranking Fuel IPW Compensation (IAT)
table.description: This is the change to the 'Cranking Fuel Injector Pulse Width (ECT)', based on the accelerator pedal.

table.name: Throttle Tip-in Enrichment A
table.description: This is the additional enrichment during throttle tip-in. Throttle tip-in is the difference between the current throttle position and the last throttle position. This enrichment represents an additional and separate firing of the injectors. The larger the value, the more fuel is potentially added. Tip-in Enrichment is not active if the thresholds, as determined by the 'Minimum Tip-in Enrichment Activation' and 'Minimum Tip-in Enrichment Activation (Throttle)' tables, are not met as well as other undefined thresholds.

table.name: Throttle Tip-in Enrichment B
table.description: This is the additional enrichment during throttle tip-in. Throttle tip-in is the difference between the current throttle position and the last throttle position. This enrichment represents an additional and separate firing of the injectors. The larger the value, the more fuel is potentially added. Tip-in Enrichment is not active if the thresholds, as determined by the 'Minimum Tip-in Enrichment Activation' and 'Minimum Tip-in Enrichment Activation (Throttle)' tables, are not met as well as other undefined thresholds.

table.name: Minimum Tip-in Enrichment Activation
table.description: Tip-in Enrichment is not active until the calculated additional IPW, as determined by the 'Throttle Tip-in Enrichment' table and with compensations applied, exceeds this value. This table does not act independently and other requirements must also be met in order for tip-in enrichment to be active.

table.name: Minimum Tip-in Enrichment Activation (Throttle)
table.description: This is the minimum throttle tip-in for active tip-in enrichment. This table does not act independently and other requirements must also be met in order for tip-in enrichment to be active.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Compensation (Boost Error)
table.description: This is the change in 'Throttle Tip-in Enrichment' based on boost error (the difference between target boost and actual boost).

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Compensation (RPM)
table.description: This is the change in 'Throttle Tip-in Enrichment' based on engine speed.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Compensation A (ECT)
table.description: This is the change in 'Throttle Tip-in Enrichment' based on coolant temperature.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Compensation B (ECT)
table.description: This is the change in 'Throttle Tip-in Enrichment' based on coolant temperature.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Compensation C (ECT)
table.description: This is the change in 'Throttle Tip-in Enrichment' based on coolant temperature.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Compensation D (ECT)
table.description: This is the change in 'Throttle Tip-in Enrichment' based on coolant temperature.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Compensation D (ECT) Activation
table.description: This is the minimum throttle tip-in for the 'Tip-in Enrichment D (ECT)' table to be active. This table does not act independently and other requirements must also be met in order for tip-in enrichment to be active.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Disable Applied Counter Threshold A (ECT)
table.description: When the applied tip-in enrichment counter is greater than or equal to this table's value, tip-in enrichment is disabled. The applied tip-in enrichment counter is incremented each time tip-in enrichment is applied and cleared when tip-in throttle is negative or the threshold in the 'Tip-in Enrichment Applied Counter Reset' table is exceeded.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Disable Applied Counter Threshold B (ECT)
table.description: When the applied tip-in enrichment counter is greater than or equal to this table's value, tip-in enrichment is disabled. The applied tip-in enrichment counter is incremented each time tip-in enrichment is applied and cleared when tip-in throttle is negative or the threshold in the 'Tip-in Enrichment Applied Counter Reset' table is exceeded.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Applied Counter Reset
table.description: When the period between tip-in enrichment application exceeds the threshold in this table, the applied tip-in enrichment counter is cleared. The period between tip-in enrichment application is a counter that is cleared when tip-in enrichment is applied and incremented when the tip-in enrichment routine is executed. The applied tip-in enrichment counter is incremented each time tip-in enrichment is applied and cleared when tip-in throttle is negative or the threshold in this table is exceeded. The applied tip-in enrichment counter is the value that is compared to the 'Tip-in Enrichment Disable Applied Counter Threshold' for disabling tip-in enrichment.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Disable Throttle Cumulative Threshold A (ECT)
table.description: When the applied tip-in enrichment cumulative throttle value is greater than or equal to this table's value, tip-in enrichment is disabled. The current throttle tip-in is added to the applied tip-in enrichment cumulative throttle value when tip-in enrichment is applied and cleared when tip-in throttle is negative or when the last applied counter threshold exceeded the 'Tip-in Throttle Cumulative Reset' threshold.

table.name: Tip-in Enrichment Disable Throttle Cumulative Threshold B (ECT)
table.description: When the applied tip-in enrichment cumulative throttle value is greater than or equal to this table's value, tip-in enrichment is disabled. The current throttle tip-in is added to the applied tip-in enrichment cumulative throttle value when tip-in enrichment is applied and cleared when tip-in throttle is negative or when the last applied counter threshold exceeded the 'Tip-in Throttle Cumulative Reset' threshold.

table.name: Tip-in Throttle Cumulative Reset
table.description: When the period between tip-in enrichment application exceeds the threshold in this table, the applied tip-in enrichment cumulative throttle value is cleared. The period between tip-in enrichment application is a counter that is cleared when tip-in enrichment is applied and incremented when the tip-in enrichment routine is executed. The current throttle tip-in is added to the applied tip-in enrichment cumulative throttle value when tip-in enrichment is applied and cleared when tip-in throttle is negative or when the last applied counter threshold is exceeded in this table. The applied tip-in enrichment cumulative throttle is the value that is compared to the 'Tip-in Enrichment Disable Applied Throttle Cumulative Threshold' for disabling tip-in enrichment.

table.name: Min Primary Base Enrichment 1 Cruise
table.description: This is one of three factors which determines the minimum primary afterstart fuel enrichment. The primary fuel enrichment is a multiplier (determined primarily by the open loop fuel maps) applied to the base injector duration to determine the level of primary enrichment. The minimum enrichment offsets determined by tables 1, 2, and 3 are added together to determine the final primary minimum enrichment. That is, regardless of the primary open loop fuel map value, enrichment will not be less than the final primary minimum enrichment. To determine an approximate minimum AFR for a particular condition, determine the final primary minimum enrichment from tables 1, 2, and 3 (adding together all three offsets) and calculate the estimated minimum AFR as 14.7/(1+x). Note: (Non-Primary OL) Enrichment 1 is added to either 1-A or 1-B (or non-cruise/cruise) to determine the final offset for table group 1, but is only added during open loop conditions not the result of the primary fuel map.

table.name: Min Primary Base Enrichment 1 Non-Cruise
table.description: This is one of three factors which determines the minimum primary afterstart fuel enrichment. The primary fuel enrichment is a multiplier (determined primarily by the open loop fuel maps) applied to the base injector duration to determine the level of primary enrichment. The minimum enrichment offsets determined by tables 1, 2, and 3 are added together to determine the final primary minimum enrichment. That is, regardless of the primary open loop fuel map value, enrichment will not be less than the final primary minimum enrichment. To determine an approximate minimum AFR for a particular condition, determine the final primary minimum enrichment from tables 1, 2, and 3 (adding together all three offsets) and calculate the estimated minimum AFR as 14.7/(1+x). Note: (Non-Primary OL) Enrichment 1 is added to either 1-A or 1-B (or non-cruise/cruise) to determine the final offset for table group 1, but is only added during open loop conditions not the result of the primary fuel map.

table.name: Min Primary Base Enrichment 1 (Non-Primary OL)_
table.description: This is one of three factors which determines the minimum primary fuel afterstart enrichment. The primary fuel enrichment is a multiplier (determined primarily by the open loop fuel maps) applied to the base injector duration to determine the level of primary enrichment. The minimum enrichment offsets determined by tables 1, 2, and 3 are added together to determine the final primary minimum enrichment. That is, regardless of the primary open loop fuel map value, enrichment will not be less than the final primary minimum enrichment. To determine an approximate minimum AFR for a particular condition, determine the final primary minimum enrichment from tables 1, 2, and 3 (adding together all three offsets) and calculate the estimated minimum AFR as 14.7/(1+x). Note: (Non-Primary OL) Enrichment 1 is added to either 1-A or 1-B (or non-cruise/cruise) to determine the final offset for table group 1, but is only added during open loop conditions not the result of the primary fuel map.

table.name: A/F Learning #1 Limits
table.description: These are the minimum and maximum ranges for A/F Learning #1. A/F Learning #1 is the long-term correction applied to fueling based on feedback from the oxygen sensor during closed loop operation.

table.name: A/F Learning #1 Airflow Ranges
table.description: These are the airflow ranges in which the different long-term fuel trims are calculated in closed loop and applied to the same airflow ranges for both closed loop and open loop.

table.name: A/F Learning Store/Apply Ranges
table.data: Max Range A / Min Range B
Max Range B / Min Range C
Max Range C / Min Range D

table.name: MAF Limit (Maximum)
table.description: This is the maximum airflow that will be used by the ECU. Airflow will be capped at this limit regardless of the airflow values in the 'MAF Sensor Scaling' table.

table.name: MAF Sensor Scaling
table.description: This is the scaling for the mass airflow sensor.

table.name: MAF Compensation (IAT)
table.description: This is the compensation of airflow based on intake temp.

table.name: Engine Load Limit A (Maximum)
table.description: This is the maximum allowable engine load. Engine load will be capped at this limit regardless of actual engine load. "Engine Load Limit B Maximum (RPM)" must also be changed as it also impacts the max engine load.

table.name: Engine Load Limit B Maximum (RPM)
table.description: This is the maximum allowable engine load. Engine load will be capped at this limit regardless of actual engine load. "Engine Load Limit A (Maximum)" must also be changed as it also impacts the max engine load.

table.name: Engine Load Compensation Cruise (MP)
table.description: This is the compensation of engine load based on RPM and manifold pressure. Cruise and non-cruise conditions are determined by a number of factors including engine speed, requested torque, MAF, vehicle speed, IAT, idle mode, ECT, and SI-Drive mode (if applicable). The ECU will ramp between the cruise and non-cruise map values when conditions dictate the switch.

table.name: Engine Load Compensation Non-Cruise (MP)
table.description: This is the compensation of engine load based on RPM and manifold pressure. Cruise and non-cruise conditions are determined by a number of factors including engine speed, requested torque, MAF, vehicle speed, IAT, idle mode, ECT, and SI-Drive mode (if applicable). The ECU will ramp between the cruise and non-cruise map values when conditions dictate the switch.

table.name: Base Timing Primary Cruise
table.description: This is the base level of timing during non-cruise conditions. Cruise and non-cruise conditions are determined by a number of factors including engine speed, requested torque, MAF, vehicle speed, IAT, idle mode, ECT, and SI-Drive mode (if applicable). The ECU will ramp between the cruise and non-cruise map values when conditions dictate the switch. The actual base timing is also determined by the 'Base Timing Reference Cruise (AVCS related)' table. During a period after initial start (related to AVCS warm-up and other factors), the ECU will calculate Base Timing as primary - min0(primary - reference), with min0 being a function limiting the (primary - reference) result to zero. If you do not want the base timing to follow this behavior, set the primary and reference maps to the same values (separated by cruise/non-cruise). Total timing = base timing + knock correction advance + other timing compensations. Knock correction advance = (knock correction advance max map value * current advance multiplier) + feedback knock correction + fine knock correction.

table.name: Base Timing Reference Cruise (AVCS related)
table.description: This is the base level of reference timing during non-cruise conditions. Cruise and non-cruise conditions are determined by a number of factors including engine speed, requested torque, MAF, vehicle speed, IAT, idle mode, ECT, and SI-Drive mode (if applicable). The ECU will ramp between the cruise and non-cruise map values when conditions dictate the switch. The actual base timing is determined as follows: during a period after initial start (related to AVCS warm-up and other factors), the ECU will calculate Base Timing as primary - min0(primary - reference), with min0 being a function limiting the (primary - reference) result to zero. If you do not want the base timing to follow this behavior, set the primary and reference maps to the same values (separated by cruise/non-cruise). Total timing = base timing + knock correction advance + other timing compensations. Knock correction advance = (knock correction advance max map value * current advance multiplier) + feedback knock correction + fine knock correction.

table.name: Base Timing Primary Non-Cruise
table.description: This is the base level of timing during non-cruise conditions. Cruise and non-cruise conditions are determined by a number of factors including engine speed, requested torque, MAF, vehicle speed, IAT, idle mode, ECT, and SI-Drive mode (if applicable). The ECU will ramp between the cruise and non-cruise map values when conditions dictate the switch. The actual base timing is also determined by the 'Base Timing Reference Non-Cruise (AVCS related)' table. During a period after initial start (related to AVCS warm-up and other factors), the ECU will calculate Base Timing as primary - min0(primary - reference), with min0 being a function limiting the (primary - reference) result to zero. If you do not want the base timing to follow this behavior, set the primary and reference maps to the same values (separated by cruise/non-cruise).Total timing = base timing + knock correction advance + other timing compensations. Knock correction advance = (knock correction advance max map value * current advance multiplier) + feedback knock correction + fine knock correction.

table.name: Base Timing Reference Non-Cruise (AVCS related)
table.description: This is the base level of reference timing during non-cruise conditions. Cruise and non-cruise conditions are determined by a number of factors including engine speed, requested torque, MAF, vehicle speed, IAT, idle mode, ECT, and SI-Drive mode (if applicable). The ECU will ramp between the cruise and non-cruise map values when conditions dictate the switch. The actual base timing is determined as follows: during a period after initial start (related to AVCS warm-up and other factors), the ECU will calculate Base Timing as primary - min0(primary - reference), with min0 being a function limiting the (primary - reference) result to zero. If you do not want the base timing to follow this behavior, set the primary and reference maps to the same values (separated by cruise/non-cruise). Total timing = base timing + knock correction advance + other timing compensations. Knock correction advance = (knock correction advance max map value * current advance multiplier) + feedback knock correction + fine knock correction.

table.name: Base Timing Idle A (In-Gear)
table.description: This is the base timing in idle mode when the transmission is not in neutral. Although the map switching between A and B is not entirely understood, it appears to be related to the TGVs.

table.name: Base Timing Idle B (In-Gear)
table.description: This is the base timing in idle mode when the transmission is not in neutral. Although the map switching between A and B is not entirely understood, it appears to be related to the TGVs.

table.name: Base Timing Idle A (Neutral)
table.description: This is the base timing in idle mode when the transmission is in neutral. Although the map switching between A and B is not entirely understood, it appears to be related to the TGVs.

table.name: Base Timing Idle B (Neutral)
table.description: This is the base timing in idle mode when the transmission is in neutral. Although the map switching between A and B is not entirely understood, it appears to be related to the TGVs.

table.name: Base Timing Idle Minimum
table.description: This is the minimum base timing in idle mode when vehicle speed is greater than the 'Base Timing Idle Minimum Vehicle Speed Enable' threshold.

table.name: Base Timing Idle Minimum Vehicle Speed Enable
table.description: The 'Base Timing Idle Minimum' table is active when vehicle speed is greater than this value.

table.name: Knock Correction Advance Max Cruise
table.description: This is the maximum amount of knock-based timing advance (knock correction advance) during cruise conditions that can be added to base timing. Cruise and non-cruise conditions are determined by a number of factors including engine speed, requested torque, MAF, vehicle speed, IAT, idle mode, ECT, and SI-Drive mode (if applicable). The ECU will ramp between the cruise and non-cruise map values when conditions dictate the switch. Total timing = base timing + knock correction advance + other timing compensations. Knock correction advance = (knock correction advance map value * IAM) + feedback knock correction + fine learning knock correction.

table.name: Knock Correction Advance Max Non-Cruise
table.description: This is the maximum amount of knock-based timing advance (knock correction advance) during non-cruise conditions that can be added to base timing. Cruise and non-cruise conditions are determined by a number of factors including engine speed, requested torque, MAF, vehicle speed, IAT, idle mode, ECT, and SI-Drive mode (if applicable). The ECU will ramp between the cruise and non-cruise map values when conditions dictate the switch. Total timing = base timing + knock correction advance + other timing compensations. Knock correction advance = (knock correction advance map value * IAM) + feedback knock correction + fine learning knock correction.

table.name: Timing Compensation A (IAT)
table.description: This is the change in total ignition timing based on input from the air intake temperature sensor.

table.name: Timing Compensation A (IAT) Activation
table.description: This is the compensation of the 'Timing Compensation A (IAT)' target according to engine speed and load.

table.name: Timing Compensation B (IAT)
table.description: This is the change in total ignition timing based on intake temperature when the knock signal is clear, the IAM is greater than the 'Timing Compensation B (IAT) IAM Activation' threshold, conditions are present where the knock sensor would be most accurate, and other factors. If a knock event occurs when this timing compensation is active, the ECU will ramp the compensation back to zero. Note: Even if this table has no compensation, compensation may still be added if the IAM is less than 1.0 and greater than the IAM activation threshold (see 'Timing Compensation B (IAT) Max Additive').

table.name: Timing Compensation B (IAT) IAM Activation
table.description: When the ignition advance multiplier (IAM) is greater than this threshold, the 'Timing Compensation B (IAT)' will potentially be active (dependent on other factors - see table help text). When the IAM is less than or equal to this threshold, this timing compensation will be set to zero.

table.name: Timing Compensation B (IAT) Max Additive
table.description: This value determines the max compensation that can be added to the current 'Timing Compensation B (IAT)' value. The ECU determines the potential additive as Knock Correction Advance Map Value - (Knock Correction Advance Map Value * IAM). This table's value limits this max additive.

table.data: Max KCA Based Additive to 'Timing Compensation B (IAT)'

table.name: Timing Compensation Imm. Cruise A (ECT)
table.description: This is the change in total ignition timing based on coolant temperature. Cruise and non-cruise conditions are determined by a number of factors including engine speed, requested torque, MAF, vehicle speed, IAT, idle mode, ECT, and SI-Drive mode (if applicable). Note: This is based on the immediate conditions related to cruise/non-cruise and results in an immediate switch, not the ramping behavior inherent with other tables when switching.

table.name: Timing Compensation Imm. Cruise B (ECT)
table.description: This is the change in total ignition timing based on coolant temperature. Cruise and non-cruise conditions are determined by a number of factors including engine speed, requested torque, MAF, vehicle speed, IAT, idle mode, ECT, and SI-Drive mode (if applicable). Note: This is based on the immediate conditions related to cruise/non-cruise and results in an immediate switch, not the ramping behavior inherent with other tables when switching.

table.name: Timing Compensation Imm. Non-Cruise A (ECT)
table.description: This is the change in total ignition timing based on coolant temperature. Cruise and non-cruise conditions are determined by a number of factors including engine speed, requested torque, MAF, vehicle speed, IAT, idle mode, ECT, and SI-Drive mode (if applicable). Note: This is based on the immediate conditions related to cruise/non-cruise and results in an immediate switch, not the ramping behavior inherent with other tables when switching.

table.name: Timing Compensation Imm. Non-Cruise B (ECT)
table.description: This is the change in total ignition timing based on coolant temperature. Cruise and non-cruise conditions are determined by a number of factors including engine speed, requested torque, MAF, vehicle speed, IAT, idle mode, ECT, and SI-Drive mode (if applicable). Note: This is based on the immediate conditions related to cruise/non-cruise and results in an immediate switch, not the ramping behavior inherent with other tables when switching.

table.name: Timing Compensation Per Cylinder A
table.description: This is the change in total ignition timing per cylinder based on RPM and engine load. It is not currently known which table corresponds to which cylinder, however it is suspected that table A corresponds to cylinder #1. When logging 'ignition timing' only cylinder A is monitored.

table.name: Timing Compensation Per Cylinder B
table.description: This is the change in total ignition timing per cylinder based on RPM and engine load. It is not currently known which table corresponds to which cylinder, however it is suspected that table A corresponds to cylinder #1. When logging 'ignition timing' only cylinder A is monitored.

table.name: Timing Compensation Per Cylinder C
table.description: This is the change in total ignition timing per cylinder based on RPM and engine load. It is not currently known which table corresponds to which cylinder, however it is suspected that table A corresponds to cylinder #1. When logging 'ignition timing' only cylinder A is monitored.

table.name: Timing Compensation Per Cylinder D
table.description: This is the change in total ignition timing per cylinder based on RPM and engine load. It is not currently known which table corresponds to which cylinder, however it is suspected that table A corresponds to cylinder #1. When logging 'ignition timing' only cylinder A is monitored.

table.name: Timing Comp Minimum Load (Per Cylinder)
table.description: The minimum load necessary in order for the 'Timing Compensation Per Cylinder' tables to be active. Active per cylinder compensation is also dependent on the 'Timing Comp Maximum RPM (Per Cylinder)' and 'Timing Comp Minimum Coolant Temp (Per Cylinder)' tables.

table.name: Timing Comp Maximum RPM (Per Cylinder)
table.description: This is the maximum engine speed for which the 'Timing Compensation Per Cylinder' tables are active. Active per cylinder compensation is also dependent on the 'Timing Comp Minimum Load (Per Cylinder)' and 'Timing Comp Minimum Coolant Temp (Per Cylinder)' tables.

table.name: Timing Comp Minimum Coolant Temp (Per Cylinder)
table.description: The minimum coolant temp necessary in order for the 'Timing Compensation Per Cylinder' tables to be active. Active per cylinder compensation is also dependent on the 'Timing Comp Maximum RPM (Per Cylinder)' and 'Timing Comp Minimum Engine Load (Per Cylinder)' tables.

table.name: Feedback Correction Range (RPM)
table.description: This is the engine speed range in which feedback corrections can be made by the ECU. Feedback correction is the immediate negative correction to timing advance due to knock as determined by the knock sensor.

table.name: Feedback Correction Minimum Load
table.description: This is the minimum engine load where feedback correction can be made by the ECU. Feedback correction is the immediate negative correction to advance due to knock as determined by the knock sensor.

table.name: Feedback Correction Retard Value
table.description: The step value for each negative adjustment to current feedback correction.

table.data: Potential Change in Current Feedback Correction Per Knock 'Event'

table.name: Feedback Correction Retard Limit
table.description: The limit for feedback correction.

table.name: Feedback Correction Negative Advance Value
table.description: When feedback correction is negative and the knock signal is then clear, feedback correction does not immediately reset to zero. Instead, the negative correction is increased by the value in this table for each time period that passes with no knock as determined by the 'Feedback Correction Negative Advance Delay' table. Feedback correction will never be greater than zero.

table.data: Change in Negative Feedback Correction After Each 'No Knock' Delay

table.name: Feedback Correction Negative Advance Delay
table.description: When feedback correction is negative, this is the delay period over which if the knock signal is clear, the negative feedback correction will be incremented by the value in the 'Feedback Correction Negative Advance Value' table. This process will continue as long as the knock signal remains clear and the delay periods are satisfied until feedback correction is zero.

table.data: 'No Knock' Delay Period for Negative Feedback Correction Advance

table.name: Extended Feedback Correction High RPM Compensation
table.description: If current feedback correction is non-zero as engine speed passes the last value in the 'Feedback Correction Range (RPM)' table, that feedback correction value continues to be applied even though engine speed is above the feedback correction disable RPM. The multiplier in this table determines the portion of that value that is applied. When engine speed drops back below the enable range, normal feedback correction activity will resume.

table.data: Feedback Correction High RPM Carry-Over Compensation

table.name: Fine Correction Range (RPM)
table.description: This is the engine speed range in which changes to the fine learning knock correction table in RAM can be potentially made by the ECU. Changes are based on knock or the relative lack of knock as determined by the knock sensor. In addition, other requirements must be met before changes can be made.

table.name: Potential Fine Correction Stored Value Adjustments
Fine Correction Range (Load)
table.description: This is the load range in which changes to the fine learning knock correction table in RAM can be potentially made by the ECU. Changes are based on knock or the relative lack of knock as determined by the knock sensor. In addition, other requirements must be met before changes can be made.

table.name: Potential Fine Correction Stored Value Adjustments
Fine Correction Rows (RPM)
table.description: These are the engine speed ranges that make up the fine learning correction table stored in RAM. These rpm ranges, as well as the load ranges specified by the 'Fine Correction Columns (Load)' table, determine how each fine correction value is stored as well as applied.

table.name: Fine Correction Columns (Load)
table.description: These are the engine load ranges that make up the fine learning correction table stored in RAM. These load ranges, as well as the rpm ranges specified by the 'Fine Correction Rows (RPM)' table, determine how each fine correction value is stored as well as applied.

table.name: Fine Correction Retard Value
table.description: The step value for each individual negative adjustment to the fine correction learning table in RAM.

table.data: Potential Change in Fine Correction Stored Value Per Knock 'Event'

table.name: Fine Correction Retard Limit
table.description: The limit for each negative fine correction learning stored value.

table.data: Fine Correction Stored Value Negative Limit

table.name: Fine Correction Advance Value
table.description: The step value for each individual positive adjustment to the fine correction learning table.

table.data: Potential Change in Fine Correction Stored Value After Each 'No Knock' Delay

table.name: Fine Correction Advance Limit
table.description: The limit for each positive fine correction learning stored value.

table.data: Fine Correction Stored Value Positive Limit

table.name: Fine Correction Advance Delay
table.description: This is the required minimum period of time with no knock, as determined by the knock sensor, before a potential positive adjustment to the fine correction learning table can be made.

table.data: 'No Knock' Delay Period for Positive Change to Fine Correction Stored Value

table.name: Rough Correction Range (RPM)
table.description: This is the engine speed range in which changes to the ignition advance multiplier (IAM) can potentially be made by the ecu. When this and other specific requirements are met, the IAM is decreased when knock is encountered, as determined by the knock sensor, or the IAM is increased with the lack of knock over a specific period of time as determined by the 'Rough Correction Learning Delay (Increasing)' table. The enable range in the 'Rough Correction Range (Load)' table must also be satisfied for potential rough correction learning.

table.name: Rough Correction Range (Load)
table.description: This is the engine load range in which changes to the ignition advance multiplier (IAM) can potentially be made by the ecu. When this and other specific requirements are met, the IAM is decreased when knock is encountered, as determined by the knock sensor, or the IAM is increased with the lack of knock over a specific period of time as determined by the 'Rough Correction Learning Delay (Increasing)' table. The enable range in the 'Rough Correction Range (RPM)' table must also be satisfied for potential rough correction learning.

table.name: Rough Correction Minimum KC Advance Map Value
table.description: This is the minimum knock correction advance max map value in order to begin re-evaluation of the IAM after entering rough correction mode. This is one of several requirements that must be met.

table.name: Rough Correction Learning Delay (Increasing)_
table.description: This is the required minimum period of time with no knock, as determined by the knock sensor, before a potential positive adjustment to the ignition advance multiplier (IAM) can be made when the IAM is being re-evaluated.

table.name: Advance Multiplier (Initial)
table.description: This is the initial value for the ignition advance multiplier (IAM). The IAM is set to this value after an ECU reset and at the beginning of a rough correction learning session where the IAM would be re-evaluated.

table.data: Post-Reset or Active Rough Correction Initial Reset Value

table.name: Advance Multiplier Step Value
table.description: This is the initial change in the ignition advance multiplier (IAM) when re-evaluation of the IAM begins during a rough correction learning session. When this starts, the IAM is reset to the 'Advance Multiplier (Initial)' value and the step value is added to or subtracted from this value depending on knock. The step value is reduced by half when, during this session, the IAM changes from increasing to decreasing, or vice versa. When the step value is 0, or the IAM hits 0 or 1.0 for a period of time, the IAM re-evaluation ends. This how the ECU determines that the IAM has settled on the appropriate value.

table.data: Initial IAM Step Value for Active Rough Correction

table.name: Intake Cam Advance Angle Cruise (AVCS)
table.description: This map selects the degree of intake cam advance for the variable valve timing system during cruise conditions. Cruise and non-cruise conditions are determined by a number of factors including engine speed, requested torque, MAF, vehicle speed, IAT, idle mode, ECT, and SI-Drive mode (if applicable). The ECU will ramp between the cruise and non-cruise map values when conditions dictate the switch.

table.name: Intake Cam Advance Angle Non-Cruise (AVCS)
table.description: This map selects the degree of intake cam advance for the variable valve timing system during non-cruise conditions. Cruise and non-cruise conditions are determined by a number of factors including engine speed, requested torque, MAF, vehicle speed, IAT, idle mode, ECT, and SI-Drive mode (if applicable). The ECU will ramp between the cruise and non-cruise map values when conditions dictate the switch.

table.name: Intake Duty Correction A
table.description: AVCS intake cam duty cycle correction based on VVT position error and engine speed.

table.name: Exhaust Duty Correction A
table.description: AVCS exhaust cam duty cycle correction based on VVT position error and engine speed.

table.name: Requested Torque (Accelerator Pedal) SI-DRIVE Sport
table.description: This table determines the driver requested torque based on the accelerator pedal angle and engine speed when SI-DRIVE Sport mode is active. This value is used to determine the target throttle plate angle as determined by the 'Target Throttle Plate Position (Requested Torque)' table.

table.name: Requested Torque (Accelerator Pedal) SI-DRIVE Sport Sharp
table.description: This table determines the driver requested torque based on the accelerator pedal angle and engine speed when SI-DRIVE Sport Sharp mode is active. This value is used to determine the target throttle plate angle as determined by the 'Target Throttle Plate Position (Requested Torque)' table.

table.name: Requested Torque (Accelerator Pedal) SI-DRIVE Intelligent
table.description: This table determines the driver requested torque based on the accelerator pedal angle and engine speed when SI-DRIVE Intelligent mode is active. This value is used to determine the target throttle plate angle as determined by the 'Target Throttle Plate Position (Requested Torque)' table.

table.name: Requested Torque Base (RPM)
table.description: The value determined by the 'Requested Torque (Accelerator Pedal)' table is divided by this table's value to determine the 'Requested Torque Accelerator Pedal to Requested Torque Base RPM' ratio. This ratio makes up the x-axis of the 'Target Throttle Plate Position (Requested Torque Ratio)' table.

table.name: Target Throttle Plate Position Cruise (Requested Torque Ratio)
table.description: This is the target throttle plate position during cruise conditions. Cruise and non-cruise conditions are determined by a number of factors including engine speed, requested torque, MAF, vehicle speed, IAT, idle mode, ECT, and SI-Drive mode (if applicable). The ECU will ramp between the cruise and non-cruise map values when conditions dictate the switch. The 'Requested Torque Accelerator Pedal to Requested Torque Base RPM (ratio)', which makes up the x-axis of this table, is the ratio of requested torque determined by the 'Requested Torque (Accelerator Pedal)' to the requested torque determined by the 'Requested Torque Base (RPM)' table. This ratio and engine speed are used to determine the target throttle plate opening.

table.name: Target Throttle Plate Position Non-Cruise (Requested Torque Ratio)
table.description: This is the target throttle plate position during non-cruise conditions. Cruise and non-cruise conditions are determined by a number of factors including engine speed, requested torque, MAF, vehicle speed, IAT, idle mode, ECT, and SI-Drive mode (if applicable). The ECU will ramp between the cruise and non-cruise map values when conditions dictate the switch. The 'Requested Torque Accelerator Pedal to Requested Torque Base RPM (ratio)', which makes up the x-axis of this table, is the ratio of requested torque determined by the 'Requested Torque (Accelerator Pedal)' to the requested torque determined by the 'Requested Torque Base (RPM)' table. This ratio and engine speed are used to determine the target throttle plate opening.

table.name: Target Throttle Plate Position Maximum (Requested Torque Ratio)
table.description: The 'Requested Torque Accelerator Pedal to Requested Torque Base RPM (ratio)', which makes up the x-axis of this table, is the ratio of requested torque determined by the 'Requested Torque (Accelerator Pedal)' to the requested torque determined by the 'Requested Torque Base (RPM)' table. This ratio and engine speed are used to determine the maximum target throttle plate opening. This maximum target throttle plate opening is used to limit the final target throttle plate opening determined by the A/B (or cruise/non-cruise) tables.

table.name: Rev Limit (Fuel Cut)
table.description: These are the engine speeds at which the rev limiter is engaged and disengaged. When engine speed is equal to or exceeds the 'On' value, fuel cut is active, after which, if engine speed drops below the 'Off' value, fueling is resumed.

table.name: Rev Limit Fuel Resume (Boost)
table.description: After the rev limiter is engaged and engine speed drops below the 'Off' RPM, fueling will not resume until manifold pressure drops below this table's value.

table.name: Speed Limiting Enable (Fuel Cut)
table.description: The speed limiter is engaged when vehicle speed is greater than this value.

table.name: Speed Limiting Disable (Fuel Cut)
table.description: The speed limiter is disengaged when vehicle speed is equal to or drops below this value after already engaging the limiter.

table.name: Speed Limiting A (Throttle) SI-DRIVE Sport/Sport Sharp
table.description: The vehicle speed at which throttle is reduced when SI-DRIVE Sport or Sport Sharp mode is active.

table.name: Speed Limiting B (Throttle) SI-DRIVE Sport/Sport Sharp
table.description: The vehicle speed at which throttle is reduced when SI-DRIVE Sport or Sport Sharp mode is active.

table.name: Speed Limiting (Throttle) SI-DRIVE Intelligent
table.description: The vehicle speed at which throttle is reduced when SI-DRIVE Intelligent mode is active.

table.name: Fuel Temp Sensor Scaling
table.description: This is the scaling for the fuel temp sensor.

table.name: Intake Temp Sensor Scaling
table.description: This is the scaling of the intake temp sensor.

table.name: Coolant Temp Sensor Scaling
table.description: This is the scaling of the coolant temp sensor.

table.name: Radiator Fan Modes A (ECT)
table.description: These are thresholds based on coolant temp which, along with the mode specified by the vehicle speed threshold table, are involved in determining radiator fan control. Radiator fan modes for coolant temp range from 0 to 2. Current mode thresholds are dependent on whether the coolant temperature is increasing or decreasing. Generally, as the coolant temp mode is higher and the vehicle speed mode is lower, the more likely the radiator fan(s) will come on. Whether the A/C is on or not also impacts the fan control. Other undefined thresholds may also change the behavior of the system.

table.name: Radiator Fan Modes B (ECT)
table.description: These are thresholds based on coolant temp which, along with the mode specified by the vehicle speed threshold table, are involved in determining radiator fan control. Radiator fan modes for coolant temp range from 0 to 2. Current mode thresholds are dependent on whether the coolant temperature is increasing or decreasing. Generally, as the coolant temp mode is higher and the vehicle speed mode is lower, the more likely the radiator fan(s) will come on. Whether the A/C is on or not also impacts the fan control. Other undefined thresholds may also change the behavior of the system.

table.name: Radiator Fan Modes (Veh. Speed)
table.description: These are thresholds based on vehicle speed which, along with the mode specified by the coolant temp threshold table, are involved in determining radiator fan control. Radiator fan modes for vehicle speed range from 0 to 3. Current mode thresholds are dependent on whether the vehicle speed is increasing or decreasing. Generally, as the coolant temp mode is higher and the vehicle speed mode is lower, the more likely the radiator fan(s) will come on. Whether the A/C is on or not also impacts the fan control. Other undefined thresholds may also change the behavior of the system.

table.name: Gear Determination Thresholds A
table.description: The ECU estimates the current gear based on rpm and vehicle speed and these are the thresholds for that determination. These should not be modified unless the transmission gear ratios have changed from the original factory set-up.

table.name: Gear Determination Thresholds B
table.description: The ECU estimates the current gear based on rpm and vehicle speed and these are the thresholds for that determination. These should not be modified unless the transmission gear ratios have changed from the original factory set-up.

table.name: Gear Determination Thresholds C
table.description: The ECU estimates the current gear based on rpm and vehicle speed and these are the thresholds for that determination. These should not be modified unless the transmission gear ratios have changed from the original factory set-up.

table.name: Idle Speed Target A
table.description: Idle speed target at different coolant temperatures.

table.name: Idle Speed Target B
table.description: Idle speed target at different coolant temperatures.

table.name: Idle Speed Target C
table.description: Idle speed target at different coolant temperatures.

table.name: Idle Speed Target D
table.description: Idle speed target at different coolant temperatures.

table.name: Calculated Engine Torque A
table.description: This table determines the expected Crank Engine Torque output relative to engine load and engine speed.

table.name: Calculated Engine Torque B
table.description: This table determines the expected Crank Engine Torque output relative to engine load and engine speed.

table.name: Calculated Engine Torque C
table.description: This table determines the expected Crank Engine Torque output relative to engine load and engine speed.

table.name: Calculated Engine Torque D
table.description: This table determines the expected Crank Engine Torque output relative to engine load and engine speed.

table.name: Low Pulse Width Fuel Injector Compensation
table.description: This compensation is applied to the injector pulsewidth when the RPM and IPW thresholds are met. It is used to compensate for nonlinear injector response at low pulsewidth. This portion of the response is known as the 'knee'

table.name: Low pulse width fuel injector compensation maximum RPM
table.description: This is the maximum RPM that the Low Pulse Width Fuel Injector Compensation is applied

table.name: Low pulse width fuel injector compensation maximum IPW
table.description: This is the maximum IPW that the Low Pulse Width Fuel Injector Compensation is applied

table.name: Ignition Dwell
table.description: UNVERIFIED - This table defines the amount of time before a spark event that the ECU needs to start charging the ignition system. Since the time needed to charge up the ignition doesn't change as engine speed increases, you need to start that event sooner to compensate for the piston moving faster.

table.name: Requested Torque Limit A (Per Gear/Engine Speed)
table.description: This table determines the Max requested torque based on calculated Gear and engine speed.

table.name: Requested Torque Limit B (Per Gear/Engine Speed)
table.description: This table determines the Max requested torque based on calculated Gear and engine speed.

table.name: Engine Load Limit B Maximum (RPM) - Multiplier
table.category: Alpha Engine Load Limit B Multiplier
table.description: Change this value to change the maximum of the Engine Load Limit B Maximum (RPM) table from it's nominal value. By setting this field to twice (x2) of it's nominal value you will increase the cap of the Engine Load Limit B Maximum (RPM) to double it's nomial value.

table.name: Fuel Pump Duty
table.description: The ECU will set fuel pump duty cycle to 0%, 100%, or one of these two values.

table.name: Tau Input A Falling Load Activation
table.description: This table determines the Tau A enrichment multiplier when engine load is decreasing.

table.name: Tau Input A Falling Load Activation A
table.description: This table determines the Tau A enrichment multiplier when engine load is decreasing.

table.name: Tau Input A Falling Load Activation B
table.description: This table determines the Tau A enrichment multiplier when engine load is decreasing.

table.name: Tau Input A Falling Load Activation C
table.description: This table determines the Tau A enrichment multiplier when engine load is decreasing.

table.name: Tau Input A Rising Load Activation
table.description: This table determines the Tau A enrichment multiplier when engine load is increasing.

table.name: Overrun Fueling RPM Resume Threshold
table.description: This 2D table determines (on an ECT x axis) at what RPM the injectors switch back on from coasting (i.e. full lean, and injector pulse width at zero). This would be useful to those with much larger injectors than stock that have a problem with stalling out as the vehicle comes to stop / idle.

table.name: Overrun Enrich RPM Delta Activation
table.description: This table sets the required RPM delta for overrun enrichment to activate.

table.name: Overrun initial injector enrichment (pulsewidth)
table.description: This table sets the initial overrun enrichment when active.

table.name: Overrun Fueling Cut Counter RPM Threshold
table.description: These counters determine the RPM count before injectors cut off during overrun.

table.name: Idle Airflow Min Target Decel Adder (RPM x ECT)
table.description: This table is an RPM by Engine Coolant table which is added to idle airflow when the minimum activation threshold based on vehicle speed is reached

table.name: Idle Airflow Min Target Decel Adder Active Veh Speed A
table.description: This table sets the activation threshold for the Idle Aifrlow Min Target Decel Adder table.

table.name: Idle Airflow Min Target Decel Initial Idle Min Airflow A
table.description: Idle airflow will be limited to a minimum value as determined by this table, for a time determined by Idle Airflow Min Target Decel Initial Idle Activation Max Mode Counter

table.name: Idle Airflow Min Target Decel Initial Idle Activation Max Mode Counter
table.description: Determines how long Idle Airflow Min Target Decel Initial Idle Min Airflow limit is applied.

table.name: Idle Airflow Min Target Decel Ramping Adder Decreasing
table.description: This table is added to target idle airflow depending on the direction of error.

table.name: Idle Airflow Min Target Decel Ramping Adder Increasing
table.description: This table is added to target idle airflow depending on the direction of error.

table.name: Timing Compensation Per Gear Activation (RPM)
table.description: This is per-gear timing compensation activation threshold by RPM.

table.name: Timing Compensation Per Gear Activation RPM
Timing Compensation Per Gear Activation (Load)
table.description: This is per-gear timing compensation activation threshold by load (g/rev).

table.name: Timing Compensation Per Gear Activation Load

table.name: Wastegate Duty Cycle Frequency
table.description: PWM frequency of the wastegate solenoid in Hz. Stock is typically 14-16 Hz. Higher frequencies reduce audible chatter.

table.name: AF 3 Correction Limits
table.description: CHANGING THIS VALUE MIN-MAX TO 0 INPUT RESULTS IN THE DISABLITY OF THE REAR O2 SENSOR INPUT ON TARGET AFR, THIS AFR TRIM 3 CAN CAUSE RICHER THAT DISIRED IDLES WHEN LEFT ON. DISABLE IF EXPERINCING RICHER THAN IDEAL IDLES WHEN ALL OTHER SCALING IS VERIFYED CORRECT OR REAR O2 SENSOR DELETE.

table.name: Map Switching Engine Speed Threshold
table.description: Engine speed threshold used in cruise/non-cruise map switching logic. When RPM exceeds this value, it contributes to transitioning toward non-cruise mode. Stock value: 2000 RPM.

table.name: Map Switching Requested Torque Ratio Threshold
table.description: Requested torque ratio threshold for cruise/non-cruise switching. When the torque ratio exceeds this value, the ECU moves toward non-cruise mode. Stock value: 0.8798.

table.name: Map Switching MAF/Load Threshold
table.description: MAF or engine load threshold for cruise/non-cruise switching. Stock value: 2.0 g/rev.

table.name: Map Switching Vehicle Speed Low Threshold
table.description: Lower vehicle speed threshold for map switching logic. Stock value: 550.

table.name: Map Switching Vehicle Speed High Threshold
table.description: Upper vehicle speed threshold for map switching logic. Stock value: 1300.

table.name: Map Switching IAT Threshold
table.description: Intake air temperature threshold for cruise/non-cruise switching. Stock value: 80 deg F.

table.name: Map Switching Idle Mode Threshold
table.description: Idle mode threshold for map switching. Stock value: 0.0 (not in idle).

table.name: Map Switching SI-Drive Mode Threshold
table.description: SI-Drive mode threshold for map switching. Stock value: 4.0.

table.name: Map Switching ECT Cold Threshold Low
table.description: Lower ECT cold threshold for map switching ratio modification. Stock value: -39 deg F.

table.name: Map Switching ECT Cold Threshold High
table.description: Upper ECT cold threshold for map switching ratio modification. Stock value: -20 deg F.

table.name: Map Switching ECT/IAT Hot Threshold A
table.description: ECT/IAT hot threshold A for map switching. Stock value: 120 deg F.

table.name: Map Switching ECT/IAT Hot Threshold B
table.description: ECT/IAT hot threshold B for map switching. Stock value: 60 deg F.

table.name: Map Switching Ratio Modifier Min
table.description: Minimum value for the map switching ratio modifier. Stock value: 0.0.

table.name: Map Switching Ratio Modifier Scale
table.description: Scale factor for the map switching ratio modifier. Stock value: 13.0.

table.name: Map Switch Ramping Adder A
table.description: Rate at which the map ratio ramps when transitioning toward cruise or non-cruise mode. Higher values = faster transitions. Stock value: 0.7.

table.name: Map Switch Ramping Adder B
table.description: Rate at which the map ratio ramps when transitioning toward cruise or non-cruise mode. Higher values = faster transitions. Stock value: 0.7.

table.name: Map Switching MAF Sensor Threshold A
table.description: MAF sensor threshold A for secondary map switching condition evaluation. Stock value: 60.0 g/s.

table.name: Map Switching MAF Sensor Threshold B
table.description: MAF sensor threshold B for secondary map switching condition evaluation. Stock value: 0.0.

table.name: Map Switching Requested Torque Min
table.description: Minimum requested torque threshold for map switching evaluation. Stock value: 5.0.

table.name: Map Switching Per-Gear RPM Threshold 1
table.description: Per-gear RPM threshold (gear 1) for map switching. All gears stock at 4000 RPM.

table.name: Map Switching Per-Gear RPM Threshold 2
table.description: Per-gear RPM threshold (gear 2) for map switching. Stock value: 4000 RPM.

table.name: Map Switching Per-Gear RPM Threshold 3
table.description: Per-gear RPM threshold (gear 3) for map switching. Stock value: 4000 RPM.

table.name: Map Switching Per-Gear RPM Threshold 4
table.description: Per-gear RPM threshold (gear 4) for map switching. Stock value: 4000 RPM.

table.name: Map Switching Per-Gear RPM Threshold 5
table.description: Per-gear RPM threshold (gear 5) for map switching. Stock value: 4000 RPM.

table.name: Map Switching Per-Gear RPM Threshold 6
table.description: Per-gear RPM threshold (gear 6) for map switching. Stock value: 4000 RPM.

table.name: Map Switching Base RPM Threshold
table.description: Base RPM threshold for secondary map switching conditions. Stock value: 2800 RPM.

table.name: Map Switching ECT Compensation A
table.description: ECT compensation value A for map switching. Stock value: 10.0.

table.name: Map Switching ECT Compensation B
table.description: ECT compensation value B for map switching. Stock value: 10.0.

table.name: Map Switching ECT Threshold
table.description: ECT threshold for secondary map switching conditions. Stock value: 69.7 deg F.

table.name: Map Switching RPM Override Threshold
table.description: RPM above which the map switching ratio is overridden. Stock value: 12000 RPM.

table.name: Map Switching Load Override Threshold
table.description: Load value above which the map switching ratio is overridden. Stock value: 5.0.

table.name: Map Switching Speed/Load Check Value
table.description: Speed/load check value for map switching. Stock value: 10.0.

table.name: Map Switching Engine Speed Hysteresis Low
table.description: Lower RPM hysteresis bound for map switching. Stock value: 7900 RPM.

table.name: Map Switching Engine Speed Hysteresis High
table.description: Upper RPM hysteresis bound for map switching. Stock value: 8000 RPM.

table.name: Map Switching Ratio Minimum Bound
table.description: Minimum bound for the map switching ratio output. Stock value: 0.0.

table.name: Map Switching Ratio Maximum Bound
table.description: Maximum bound for the map switching ratio output. Stock value: 20.0.

table.name: Map Switching Percentage Ceiling
table.description: Percentage ceiling for map switching calculation. Stock value: 100.0.

table.name: Map Switching Cruise Switch Counter A
table.description: Delay counter A for cruise/non-cruise switching. Controls how many scheduler cycles before the switch is committed. Stock value: 50.

table.name: Map Switching Cruise Switch Min Delay A
table.description: Minimum delay A before cruise/non-cruise switch is permitted. Stock value: 38.

table.name: Map Switching Cruise Switch Counter B
table.description: Delay counter B for cruise/non-cruise switching. Stock value: 100.

table.name: Map Switching Cruise Switch Min Delay B
table.description: Minimum delay B before cruise/non-cruise switch is permitted. Stock value: 38.

table.name: Timing Blend Lookup Input Threshold
table.description: Input threshold for the timing blend lookup. Stock value: 10.0.

table.name: Timing Blend RPM Activation Threshold
table.description: RPM threshold above which timing blending becomes active. Stock value: 700 RPM.

table.name: Timing Blend RPM Secondary Threshold
table.description: Secondary RPM threshold for timing blend logic. Stock value: 600 RPM.

table.name: Timing Blend IAT Threshold A
table.description: IAT threshold A for timing blend calculations. Stock value: 69.65 deg F.

table.name: Timing Blend IAT Threshold B
table.description: IAT threshold B for timing blend calculations. Stock value: 69.65 deg F.

table.name: Timing Blend Correction Offset
table.description: Correction offset applied during timing blending. Stock value: -3.0 degrees.

table.name: Timing Blend RPM Max for Blending
table.description: Maximum RPM for timing blend operation. Stock value: 10000 RPM.

table.name: Timing Blend Ramping Rate
table.description: Rate/factor at which the timing blend ratio changes per cycle. Stock value: 0.35.

table.name: Timing Blend Correction Threshold
table.description: Correction threshold for timing blend calculations. Stock value: 44.0 degrees.

table.name: Timing Blend Half Ratio (Map Ratio E42)
table.description: Half-ratio value used in timing blend. Likely the Map Ratio E42 reference value. Stock value: 0.5.

table.name: Timing Blend Minimum Ratio
table.description: Minimum ratio value for timing blend. Stock value: 0.0.

table.name: Timing Blend RPM Limit
table.description: RPM limit for timing blend operation. Stock value: 6000 RPM.

table.name: Timing Blend Ratio Floor
table.description: Floor value for the timing blend ratio. Stock value: 0.0.

table.name: Timing Blend Ratio Ceiling
table.description: Ceiling value for the timing blend ratio. Stock value: 1.0.
